steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The skill is Boundaries (punctuation). The sentence has the structure: introductory prepositional phrase (“By supplying grain, textiles, and tin to societies that lacked these valuable items”) followed by the main clause (“the Neo-Assyrian Empire grew …”). A comma is required to separate an introductory phrase from the main clause.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "items. The": A period creates two sentences, but “By supplying … items” has no main verb — it is a prepositional phrase, not an independent clause. This produces a sentence fragment. ✗
  • "items the": Without any punctuation, the introductory phrase runs directly into the main clause with no boundary signal, making the sentence difficult to parse. Standard English requires a comma after an introductory phrase. ✗
  • "items; the": A semicolon must connect two independent clauses. “By supplying … items” is a prepositional phrase, not an independent clause, so a semicolon is grammatically incorrect here. ✗
  • "items, the": A comma correctly closes the introductory prepositional phrase and signals the start of the main clause “the Neo-Assyrian Empire grew …” This conforms to Standard English conventions. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
